title: EV batteries
subtitle: Lithium and nickel through cathode, cell and pack to the automaker, and the handful of firms the whole chain buys from.
financials_note: Five-year income-statement trends for the chokepoints. Revenue bars read on the left; margins read on the right. Several chokepoints here are Chinese A-share or Beijing Stock Exchange filers reporting in RMB, so expect lower source tiers than a map of SEC filers, and read the tier badge before trusting a number.
chokepoints_note: The companies the rest of the chain has the fewest alternatives to. Cell assembly and vehicle making are comparatively diverse; the materials layers beneath them, separator film, graphite anode and electrolyte salt, are where the routing options run out. Every call here is a foundation hypothesis until relationship edges test it.

layers.label: Miners
layers.desc: Pull lithium, nickel, cobalt and graphite out of the ground

layers.label: Refiners
layers.desc: Turn ore and brine into battery grade chemicals

layers.label: Cathode makers
layers.desc: Build the positive electrode material and its precursor

layers.label: Anode makers
layers.desc: Purify and graphitize the negative electrode material

layers.label: Electrolyte makers
layers.desc: Formulate the electrolyte and the lithium salt it carries

layers.label: Separator makers
layers.desc: Coat the precision film that keeps the electrodes apart

layers.label: Cell makers
layers.desc: Assemble the finished cells and packs

layers.label: Automakers
layers.desc: Buy the packs and build the vehicles

layers.label: Recyclers
layers.desc: Recover metal from scrap and spent cells

chokepoint_why.CATL: The world's largest EV cell maker, over a third of global output on its own, and most non-Chinese automakers rely on it for at least one platform. The single hardest node to route around in this chain.
chokepoint_why.BYD: The second largest cell maker and the only one that is also a top tier automaker, so it does not depend on the rest of the chain the way other cell makers do. Vertically integrated from cells to vehicles, which makes it a chokepoint on the supply and the demand side at once.
chokepoint_why.BTR New Energy Materials: The world's largest graphite anode producer. Anode purification and graphitization is almost entirely a Chinese industrial capability today, and China's late 2023 graphite export permit requirement showed how exposed the rest of the chain is to it.
chokepoint_why.Asahi Kasei: Owns Celgard and Hipore, and is the main non-Chinese alternative in separator film. One of a handful of firms whose separator technology underpins the industry.
chokepoint_why.Yunnan Energy New Material (SEMCORP): The largest separator maker globally by shipment volume, in a country that holds the large majority of separator capacity. Separator film is low margin, capital intensive precision coating, so entry is slow and a quality or export problem here stalls cell output industry wide.
chokepoint_why.Guangzhou Tinci Materials Technology: The largest electrolyte maker globally, and a major source of the LiPF6 salt nearly every electrolyte formulation uses. LiPF6 production is itself concentrated among a small number of Chinese chemical makers.
chokepoint_why.Glencore: One of the largest cobalt producers and traders, and a top operator in the DRC, where global cobalt supply is heavily concentrated. Its position makes DRC political and infrastructure risk a chain wide risk.
chokepoint_why.CMOC Group: The Chinese controlled miner that overtook Glencore as the world's top cobalt producer through its DRC operations at Tenke Fungurume and Kisanfu. The other half of the cobalt concentration story.
